summary: Set 031 Session 2 (final): per-agent pointers + 0.5.0 PyPI release + cross-repo notification + close-out. Shipped: ai_router/CHANGELOG.md 0.5.0 entry summarizing the full Set 031 deliverable surface (Session 1's schema + validator + journal + workflow doc + 33 tests, plus Session 2's pointer blocks + version bump + gitignore); pyproject.toml + ai_router/__init__.py bump 0.4.0 -> 0.5.0; .gitignore excludes ai_router/consensus-decisions/ (full-payload dir; JSONL stays committed per router-metrics.jsonl precedent); byte-identical 'Decision-time consensus (pointer)' block added to this repo's CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md + GEMINI.md and to dabbler-platform/CLAUDE.md + dabbler-access-harvester/CLAUDE.md (healthcare-accessdb skipped - Lightweight tier with no local workflow doc); PyPI release shipped via tag-driven OIDC trusted publishing (workflow run 26128035426, classify+build+publish all green, operator-approved `pypi` environment); verified live at pypi.org/project/dabbler-ai-router/0.5.0/. Round-A verification (gemini-pro $0.008) flagged ONE Major: CHANGELOG 'Consumer-repo notification' bullet used past tense at release time when cross-repo writes are operator-gated AFTER PyPI publish; fix landed (bullet rephrased to remove past-tense over-claim and to note consumer adoption doesn't require the edits). Round-B (gemini-pro $0.007) APPROVE. Operator override at S2 start: ran as Claude Opus 4.7 @ high instead of S1 disposition's sonnet-medium recommendation; reasoning headroom kept available for PyPI release surprises per spec R4. Total S2 verifier cost $0.031 (incl. $0.009 wasted on a bash-vs-PowerShell env-var syntax mix-up that ran the re-run as Round-A again before being corrected). Cumulative Set 031 spend $0.051 of $5.00 NTE - 1% utilization, well below the spec's $0.07-$0.25 forecast lower bound. Set 031 closes here; the orchestrator-side consensus-wiring is deferred to a follow-on session set (this set ships V1 schema only). Cross-repo writes left uncommitted in dabbler-platform + dabbler-access-harvester per operator's 'I write all three; you commit/push' selection.
